message:
fix(http/h1): reorder chunked trailer size limit check to avoid premature HeadersTooLarge rejection

Move the max_trailers_size check in both ClientIncomingBody (client.rs)
and IncomingBodyWriter (stream.rs) to AFTER attempting to find a
complete trailer line (\r\n) in the buffer. Previously, the size check
ran before confirming a complete line was present, which could cause a
premature HeadersTooLarge rejection when buffered data had not yet
formed a complete trailer line.

The terminal \r\n (empty line) that signals the end of the trailer
section is no longer counted against the trailer size limit, since it
is a framing delimiter rather than trailer content.

Additionally, in stream.rs, fix OutgoingBody to set self.done = true
immediately when a trailers frame is received. This ensures that
is_end_stream() returns true right after trailers are provided,
without requiring an additional poll cycle.

New tests:
- decode_chunked_response_trailer_limit_does_not_count_terminal_crlf
  (client.rs)
- incoming_body_chunked_trailer_limit_does_not_count_terminal_crlf
  (stream.rs)
- outgoing_body_trailers_mark_end_stream_immediately (stream.rs)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
